usage: add_via [-help] [-net <net>] -pt {x y} [-shape {blockring blockagewire blockwire corewire drcfill fillopc fillwire followpin iowire none padring ring stripe}] [-shield_net <net>] [-status {routed fixed cover shield}] [-user_class <value>] -via <via_def>

options.name: -net
options.description: The name or pointer of the net to associate with the via instance. If not specified, the via will be assigned as a floating fillwire via. ((net), optional)

options.name: -pt
options.description: The placement location (in microns) of the via instance.

options.name: -shape
options.description: {blockring blockagewire blockwire corewire drcfill fillopc fillwire followpin iowire none padring ring stripe} Special route shape (DEF SPECIALNETS wiring+ SHAPE) specification. If not given, the default is none, unless -net is not specified in which case the shape will be set to fillwire.

options.name: -shield_net
options.description: The name or pointer of the net to be shielded by the via instance. Only valid when -status is set to shield. ((net), optional)

options.name: -status
options.description: Special route (DEF SPECIALNETS) wiring status. If not specified, the status is set to routed.

options.name: -user_class
options.description: User defined string to be added to the via instance that is created.

options.name: -via
options.description: The via-master name or pointer of the via master to place. ((viaDef), required)
